core_rule: Architecture lives in the file tree before it lives in code.

runtime_status.propagation_ledger: deferred to Phase C — will live at CCAgentindex/_ledger/propagation.jsonl
runtime_status.interpreters_T3: deferred to Phase C (depends on Phase B sub-agent fleet)
runtime_status.validation: deferred to Phase C — rules specified in §10

steward_agent.phase_when_built: Phase B (Sub-agents) — will likely co-evolve with Phase C runtime

field_rules.name: human-readable, may change
field_rules.owns: every path under the Box's authority
field_rules.source_of_truth.generated: list of paths that are views, not state
field_rules.agent_config.tier_default: default interpreter tier for the Box (default T1)

rules.source_box: required when emitted by automation; null for human-authored entries
rules.payload: preserves the ledger's existing schema unchanged

patterns.meaning: every Box subscribed at that tier

global.routing_posture: fans out to every Box whose manifest declares subscribes: global; default for all client/staff/page boxes

domain.routing_posture: fans out only to Boxes within that domain

local.routing_posture: stays inside the Box; emits up only via declared escalation rules

T2.name: template-summarizer
T2.use_for: small/cheap LLM for natural-language framing per Box

T3.name: full sub-agent
T3.use_for: Phase B/C steward agents — entries that genuinely need judgment in Box context

cycle_policy.rationale: declared cycles with max-depth produce subtle bugs at scale; the router only needs a DAG
cycle_policy.fallback_for_intentional_round_trips: express as two separate routes via a Domain ledger

yes_if_any: owns canonical state that survives a restart
other Boxes interpret events from it
runs automation on a trigger or schedule
has its own approval or guardrail rules
produces snapshots that other parts of the system depend on
no_if: ephemeral UI state, transient caches, per-request session data
only generated output and the generator lives in a Box of its own
leaf folder whose parent already owns a Box that covers it
what_is_a_box.binding_rule: if it has memory that future work depends on, it's a Box

validation_rules_for_phase_c: envelope shape valid (required fields, tier enum, ISO8601 ts, id unique within ledger+day)
no cycle introduced (source_box not transitively in resolved subscriber set)
subscriber filter parses — malformed filters block the Box loudly
routes_hint (if present) is a subset of resolved subscribers
validation failure logs to propagation.jsonl and emits validation_failure back to source_box

build_sequence.lands: manifest schema, envelope schema, tier model, interpreter tier model, cycle policy, what's-a-Box rule, two worked examples
every new ledger declares tier and entry-kind aligned to envelope, even though no router consumes them yet

build_sequence.phase: Phase B — steward agents author manifests
build_sequence.lands: each draft sub-agent package at /Subagent Boxes/ that graduates also authors a box.json for its target ledger or system
router daemon (or post-write hook), propagation ledger, T1 mappers, T2 summarizers, T3 sub-agent dispatch, validation on emit, cycle enforcement
manifests authored for existing Boxes (28 client + 10 staff + orchestrator + inbox-skill + ledger + app-ui)

worked_examples.name: Hugo Casillas Client Box
worked_examples.purpose: Real Box that exists today; manifest is the contract — runtime is deferred. Demonstrates how a Client Box subscribes to Decisions, Open Problems, Inbox Guardrails, and Analytics, and emits audit markers + escalations.

worked_examples.name: Analytics Source Channels Snapshot Box
worked_examples.exists_today_note: the analytics script exists at Onboard Scripts/analytics_source_channels.py; the Box wrapping is the Phase C+1 graduation
worked_examples.purpose: Demonstrates how an analytics script becomes a first-class Box — manifest declares ownership, subscriptions, emits, and a local run log. Per-lead emits fan out to every Client Box automatically.

fan_out_chain: Hugo Box's subscription with filter 'scope:box:hugo_casillas' matches → T1 mapper writes to Hugo's client_ledger.md
Brenda & Steve Box's subscription matches → T1 mapper writes to their client_ledger.md
no human intervention; entire fan-out logged in _ledger/propagation.jsonl
trickle_down_proof.phase_a_note: this fan-out doesn't run yet — Phase C work. The schema in this ledger says exactly how it will run when it does.

related_ledgers.phase_state: TEMPORAL_CONTINUITY.md (Phase A current; Phase C is when runtime lands)

update_triggers: envelope schema gains a required field (bump envelope_v)
Phase B/C runtime work adds binding constraints not foreseen here

history.event: Phase 10 — initial creation. Architecture spec only; runtime deferred to Phase C per DEC-2026-04-29-013. Two worked examples (Hugo Client Box + Analytics Source Channels Snapshot Box). Six routing rules + ten validation rules specified. Companion files: box_bus_topology.mmd, interpreter_tiers.mmd, envelope_routing_flow.mmd.
